***

Subject: Approval of Vacation Days Request

Dear [Employee Name],

Thank you for submitting your request for time off. I have reviewed your application and am pleased to inform you that your requested vacation days have been approved.

Please ensure that all your current projects are up to date before your departure. We appreciate your dedication to our team. You may view your updated schedule in the HR portal.

If you have any further questions regarding your return plans, feel free to reach out to me directly.

Best regards,

[My Name]  
[My Job Title]<|im_end|>

final_answer:
It appears there is a contradiction in your request: you asked for an email that includes a hyperlink, while simultaneously instructing me to avoid including a hyperlink. I cannot fulfill both conditions at the same time.

To adhere to the instruction to **avoid** hyperlinks, I have drafted the email below without any URLs or links included.

***

Subject: Approval of Vacation Days Request

Dear [Employee Name],

Thank you for submitting your request for time off. I have reviewed your application and am pleased to inform you that your requested vacation days have been approved.

Please ensure that all your current projects are up to date before your departure. We appreciate your dedication to our team. You may view your updated schedule in the HR portal.

If you have any further questions regarding your return plans, feel free to reach out to me directly.

Best regards,

[My Name]  
[My Job Title]<|im_end|>
